title: Backend and Data Interview Question Bank
subtitle: Bộ câu hỏi backend trung cấp về API, lưu trữ, validation và reliability.

questions.question: Vì sao nên validation request happen near the API boundary?
questions.intent: Checks whether you isolate bad input early.
options: To reject invalid các payload before deeper logic nghiệp vụ runs.
B. To make every request slower.
C. Bởi vì databases cannot store strings.
questions.correct_answer: To reject invalid các payload before deeper logic nghiệp vụ runs.
questions.explanation: Boundary validation reduces downstream error states, keeps contracts explicit, and prevents invalid data from leaking into core logic or persistence layers.
questions.code_snippet: if not payload.get('email'):\n    raise ValueError('email is required')
compare: Early validation: clearer failure and safer internal code.
Late validation: more nhánh xử lý and harder debugging.
questions.question_en: Why should request validation happen near the API boundary?
questions.question_vi: Vì sao nên validation request happen near the API boundary?
questions.correct_answer_en: A. To reject invalid payloads before deeper business logic runs.
questions.correct_answer_vi: To reject invalid các payload before deeper logic nghiệp vụ runs.
questions.explanation_en: Boundary validation reduces downstream error states, keeps contracts explicit, and prevents invalid data from leaking into core logic or persistence layers.
questions.explanation_vi: Boundary validation reduces downstream error states, keeps contracts explicit, and prevents invalid data from leaking into core logic or persistence layers.
options_en: A. To reject invalid payloads before deeper business logic runs.
B. To make every request slower.
C. Because databases cannot store strings.
options_vi: To reject invalid các payload before deeper logic nghiệp vụ runs.
B. To make every request slower.
C. Bởi vì databases cannot store strings.
compare_en: Early validation: clearer failure and safer internal code.
Late validation: more branching and harder debugging.
compare_vi: Early validation: clearer failure and safer internal code.
Late validation: more nhánh xử lý and harder debugging.
questions.intent_en: Checks whether you isolate bad input early.
questions.intent_vi: Checks whether you isolate bad input early.

questions.question: Transaction and why is it useful là gì?
questions.intent: Checks whether you understand atomic multi-step changes.
options: Transaction groups operations so they succeed or fail together.
B. Transaction is only a SQL comment.
C. Transaction guarantees infinite scaling.
questions.correct_answer: Transaction groups operations so they succeed or fail together.
questions.explanation: Transactions help preserve data consistency. If one step fails, the system can roll back the whole đơn vị công việc instead of leaving partial writes.
compare: Transaction: safer for coupled writes.
No transaction: partial state is more likely on failure.
questions.question_en: What is a transaction and why is it useful?
questions.question_vi: Transaction and why is it useful là gì?
questions.correct_answer_en: A. A transaction groups operations so they succeed or fail together.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Transaction groups operations so they succeed or fail together.
questions.explanation_en: Transactions help preserve data consistency. If one step fails, the system can roll back the whole unit of work instead of leaving partial writes.
questions.explanation_vi: Transactions help preserve data consistency. If one step fails, the system can roll back the whole đơn vị công việc instead of leaving partial writes.
options_en: A. A transaction groups operations so they succeed or fail together.
B. A transaction is only a SQL comment.
C. A transaction guarantees infinite scaling.
options_vi: Transaction groups operations so they succeed or fail together.
B. Transaction is only a SQL comment.
C. Transaction guarantees infinite scaling.
compare_en: Transaction: safer for coupled writes.
No transaction: partial state is more likely on failure.
compare_vi: Transaction: safer for coupled writes.
No transaction: partial state is more likely on failure.
questions.intent_en: Checks whether you understand atomic multi-step changes.
questions.intent_vi: Checks whether you understand atomic multi-step changes.

questions.question: When does caching help a backend service?
questions.intent: Tests whether you know why and when to trade độ tươi mới for speed.
options: When các lần đọc tốn kém lặp đi lặp lại can tolerate some staleness.
B. For every write-heavy workflow automatically.
C. Only when the database is broken.
questions.correct_answer: When các lần đọc tốn kém lặp đi lặp lại can tolerate some staleness.
questions.explanation: Caching reduces latency and backend load, but it introduces invalidation and độ tươi mới tradeoffs. Nó giúp most when the same data is read often and changes less frequently.
questions.code_snippet:
cached = cache.get(key)
if cached is not None:
    return cached
value = load_from_db(key)
cache.set(key, value, ttl=60)
compare: Cache hit: lower latency and lower DB load.
Cache miss or cũ cache: extra complexity and potential inconsistency.
questions.question_en: When does caching help a backend service?
questions.question_vi: When does caching help a backend service?
questions.correct_answer_en: A. When repeated expensive reads can tolerate some staleness.
questions.correct_answer_vi: When các lần đọc tốn kém lặp đi lặp lại can tolerate some staleness.
questions.explanation_en: Caching reduces latency and backend load, but it introduces invalidation and freshness tradeoffs. It helps most when the same data is read often and changes less frequently.
questions.explanation_vi: Caching reduces latency and backend load, but it introduces invalidation and độ tươi mới tradeoffs. Nó giúp most when the same data is read often and changes less frequently.
options_en: A. When repeated expensive reads can tolerate some staleness.
B. For every write-heavy workflow automatically.
C. Only when the database is broken.
options_vi: When các lần đọc tốn kém lặp đi lặp lại can tolerate some staleness.
B. For every write-heavy workflow automatically.
C. Only when the database is broken.
compare_en: Cache hit: lower latency and lower DB load.
Cache miss or stale cache: extra complexity and potential inconsistency.
compare_vi: Cache hit: lower latency and lower DB load.
Cache miss or cũ cache: extra complexity and potential inconsistency.
questions.intent_en: Tests whether you know why and when to trade freshness for speed.
questions.intent_vi: Tests whether you know why and when to trade độ tươi mới for speed.

questions.question: Vì sao sự tiện lợi của ORM vẫn đòi hỏi kiến thức SQL?
questions.intent: Checks whether you understand abstractions do not remove query cost.
options: Các query do ORM sinh ra vẫn có thể kém hiệu quả hoặc không phù hợp với workload.
B. ORMs replace all need for data modeling.
C. SQL is unrelated to relational databases.
questions.correct_answer: Các query do ORM sinh ra vẫn có thể kém hiệu quả hoặc không phù hợp với workload.
questions.explanation: Bạn vẫn cần hiểu join, index và hình dạng của query. Nếu không, ORM có thể che giấu các N+1 query chậm hoặc kéo về nhiều dữ liệu hơn mức cần thiết.
questions.code_snippet: users = session.query(User).all()\nfor user in users:\n    print(user.orders)  # may trigger N+1 queries
compare: ORM: faster development and cleaner models.
Raw SQL insight: necessary for performance and debugging.
questions.question_en: Why can ORM convenience still require SQL knowledge?
questions.question_vi: Vì sao sự tiện lợi của ORM vẫn đòi hỏi kiến thức SQL?
questions.correct_answer_en: A. ORM-generated queries can still be inefficient or incorrect for the workload.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Các query do ORM sinh ra vẫn có thể kém hiệu quả hoặc không phù hợp với workload.
questions.explanation_en: You still need to understand joins, indexes, and query shape. Otherwise, an ORM can hide slow N+1 queries or pull much more data than needed.
questions.explanation_vi: Bạn vẫn cần hiểu join, index và hình dạng của query. Nếu không, ORM có thể che giấu các N+1 query chậm hoặc kéo về nhiều dữ liệu hơn mức cần thiết.
options_en: A. ORM-generated queries can still be inefficient or incorrect for the workload.
B. ORMs replace all need for data modeling.
C. SQL is unrelated to relational databases.
options_vi: Các query do ORM sinh ra vẫn có thể kém hiệu quả hoặc không phù hợp với workload.
B. ORMs replace all need for data modeling.
C. SQL is unrelated to relational databases.
compare_en: ORM: faster development and cleaner models.
Raw SQL insight: necessary for performance and debugging.
compare_vi: ORM: faster development and cleaner models.
Raw SQL insight: necessary for performance and debugging.
questions.intent_en: Checks whether you understand abstractions do not remove query cost.
questions.intent_vi: Checks whether you understand abstractions do not remove query cost.

questions.question: Vì sao nên external API failures be isolated from core domain logic?
questions.intent: Tests whether you can limit blast radius.
options: So sự bất ổn của upstream does not crash unrelated business flows.
B. So the codebase has more folders.
C. Bởi vì external APIs are always optional.
questions.correct_answer: So sự bất ổn của upstream does not crash unrelated business flows.
questions.explanation: Separating providers behind an adapter or service ranh giới makes timeouts, retries, fallbacks, and test double easier to manage without contaminating the rest of the system.
compare: Isolated adapter: easier testing and controlled retries.
Provider logic everywhere: harder to change and debug.
questions.question_en: Why should external API failures be isolated from core domain logic?
questions.question_vi: Vì sao nên external API failures be isolated from core domain logic?
questions.correct_answer_en: A. So upstream instability does not crash unrelated business flows.
questions.correct_answer_vi: So sự bất ổn của upstream does not crash unrelated business flows.
questions.explanation_en: Separating providers behind an adapter or service boundary makes timeouts, retries, fallbacks, and test doubles easier to manage without contaminating the rest of the system.
questions.explanation_vi: Separating providers behind an adapter or service ranh giới makes timeouts, retries, fallbacks, and test double easier to manage without contaminating the rest of the system.
options_en: A. So upstream instability does not crash unrelated business flows.
B. So the codebase has more folders.
C. Because external APIs are always optional.
options_vi: So sự bất ổn của upstream does not crash unrelated business flows.
B. So the codebase has more folders.
C. Bởi vì external APIs are always optional.
compare_en: Isolated adapter: easier testing and controlled retries.
Provider logic everywhere: harder to change and debug.
compare_vi: Isolated adapter: easier testing and controlled retries.
Provider logic everywhere: harder to change and debug.
questions.intent_en: Tests whether you can limit blast radius.
questions.intent_vi: Tests whether you can limit blast radius.

questions.question: Which is usually a backend anti-pattern for loading related data: một query cho mỗi dòng cha or một query được thiết kế phù hợp that fetches needed relations eagerly?
options: One query per parent row is the anti-pattern bởi vì it often creates N+1 queries.
B. The shaped query is always slower by definition.
C. Databases optimize both into identical thực thi plans automatically.
questions.correct_answer: One query per parent row is the anti-pattern bởi vì it often creates N+1 queries.
questions.explanation: Repeated relation loading causes avoidable các vòng round trip and query overhead. Loading strategy should match the actual access pattern.
compare: N+1 loading: simple to write, expensive at scale.
Planned eager loading: often better for known access paths.
questions.question_en: Which is usually a backend anti-pattern for loading related data: one query per parent row or one shaped query that fetches needed relations eagerly?
questions.question_vi: Which is usually a backend anti-pattern for loading related data: một query cho mỗi dòng cha or một query được thiết kế phù hợp that fetches needed relations eagerly?
questions.correct_answer_en: A. One query per parent row is the anti-pattern because it often creates N+1 queries.
questions.correct_answer_vi: One query per parent row is the anti-pattern bởi vì it often creates N+1 queries.
questions.explanation_en: Repeated relation loading causes avoidable round trips and query overhead. Loading strategy should match the actual access pattern.
questions.explanation_vi: Repeated relation loading causes avoidable các vòng round trip and query overhead. Loading strategy should match the actual access pattern.
options_en: A. One query per parent row is the anti-pattern because it often creates N+1 queries.
B. The shaped query is always slower by definition.
C. Databases optimize both into identical execution plans automatically.
options_vi: One query per parent row is the anti-pattern bởi vì it often creates N+1 queries.
B. The shaped query is always slower by definition.
C. Databases optimize both into identical thực thi plans automatically.
compare_en: N+1 loading: simple to write, expensive at scale.
Planned eager loading: often better for known access paths.
compare_vi: N+1 loading: simple to write, expensive at scale.
Planned eager loading: often better for known access paths.

questions.question: Performance trap in selecting `*` from a large table when only two fields are needed là gì?
options: Extra columns increase transfer, parsing, and memory cost.
B. SQL does not allow selecting fewer fields in production.
C. Wide rows always compress to the same size anyway.
questions.correct_answer: Extra columns increase transfer, parsing, and memory cost.
questions.explanation: Selecting only the các cột cần thiết is a simple optimization that can matter significantly on large datasets or hot endpoints.
questions.code_snippet: SELECT id, email FROM users WHERE status = 'active';
compare: SELECT *: convenient but often wasteful.
Projected columns: leaner for bandwidth and memory.
questions.question_en: What is the performance trap in selecting `*` from a large table when only two fields are needed?
questions.question_vi: Performance trap in selecting `*` from a large table when only two fields are needed là gì?
questions.correct_answer_en: A. Extra columns increase transfer, parsing, and memory cost.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Extra columns increase transfer, parsing, and memory cost.
questions.explanation_en: Selecting only the required columns is a simple optimization that can matter significantly on large datasets or hot endpoints.
questions.explanation_vi: Selecting only the các cột cần thiết is a simple optimization that can matter significantly on large datasets or hot endpoints.
options_en: A. Extra columns increase transfer, parsing, and memory cost.
B. SQL does not allow selecting fewer fields in production.
C. Wide rows always compress to the same size anyway.
options_vi: Extra columns increase transfer, parsing, and memory cost.
B. SQL does not allow selecting fewer fields in production.
C. Wide rows always compress to the same size anyway.
compare_en: SELECT *: convenient but often wasteful.
Projected columns: leaner for bandwidth and memory.
compare_vi: SELECT *: convenient but often wasteful.
Projected columns: leaner for bandwidth and memory.

questions.question: Vì sao caching có thể thay đổi database-derived objects and then mutating them tại chỗ a trap?
options: Other các request may observe cũ or bị sửa đổi ngoài dự kiến cached state.
B. Python automatically deep-copies all cached values.
C. Cached objects cannot be mutated after retrieval.
questions.correct_answer: Other các request may observe cũ or bị sửa đổi ngoài dự kiến cached state.
questions.explanation: Caches need careful control over object identity, mutation, serialization, and invalidation.
questions.code_snippet: profile = cache.get(key)\nprofile['count'] += 1  # risky if shared mutable cache object
compare: Mutable shared cache objects: risky and surprising.
Immutable/serialized cache values: safer boundaries.
questions.question_en: Why is caching mutable database-derived objects and then mutating them in-place a trap?
questions.question_vi: Vì sao caching có thể thay đổi database-derived objects and then mutating them tại chỗ a trap?
questions.correct_answer_en: A. Other requests may observe stale or unexpectedly modified cached state.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Other các request may observe cũ or bị sửa đổi ngoài dự kiến cached state.
questions.explanation_en: Caches need careful control over object identity, mutation, serialization, and invalidation.
questions.explanation_vi: Caches need careful control over object identity, mutation, serialization, and invalidation.
options_en: A. Other requests may observe stale or unexpectedly modified cached state.
B. Python automatically deep-copies all cached values.
C. Cached objects cannot be mutated after retrieval.
options_vi: Other các request may observe cũ or bị sửa đổi ngoài dự kiến cached state.
B. Python automatically deep-copies all cached values.
C. Cached objects cannot be mutated after retrieval.
compare_en: Mutable shared cache objects: risky and surprising.
Immutable/serialized cache values: safer boundaries.
compare_vi: Mutable shared cache objects: risky and surprising.
Immutable/serialized cache values: safer boundaries.

questions.question: Vì sao returning exception nội bộ thô directly to API clients a bad idea?
options: It can leak internals and produce unstable hướng tới client contracts.
B. Clients prefer stack traces for all failures.
C. It makes performance benchmarking easier.
questions.correct_answer: It can leak internals and produce unstable hướng tới client contracts.
questions.explanation: Client-facing errors should be deliberate, stable, and safe rather than accidental reflections of internal implementation.
compare: Raw internal exception: unstable and potentially unsafe.
Mapped API error contract: cleaner for clients.
questions.question_en: Why is returning raw internal exceptions directly to API clients a bad idea?
questions.question_vi: Vì sao returning exception nội bộ thô directly to API clients a bad idea?
questions.correct_answer_en: A. It can leak internals and produce unstable client-facing contracts.
questions.correct_answer_vi: It can leak internals and produce unstable hướng tới client contracts.
questions.explanation_en: Client-facing errors should be deliberate, stable, and safe rather than accidental reflections of internal implementation.
questions.explanation_vi: Client-facing errors should be deliberate, stable, and safe rather than accidental reflections of internal implementation.
options_en: A. It can leak internals and produce unstable client-facing contracts.
B. Clients prefer stack traces for all failures.
C. It makes performance benchmarking easier.
options_vi: It can leak internals and produce unstable hướng tới client contracts.
B. Clients prefer stack traces for all failures.
C. It makes performance benchmarking easier.
compare_en: Raw internal exception: unstable and potentially unsafe.
Mapped API error contract: cleaner for clients.
compare_vi: Raw internal exception: unstable and potentially unsafe.
Mapped API error contract: cleaner for clients.

questions.question: Trap in checking uniqueness only in application code before insert and not enforcing it in the database là gì?
options: Các request đồng thời vẫn có thể chèn dữ liệu trùng lặp.
B. Databases reject duplicates automatically without constraints.
C. Application checks are always stronger than constraints.
questions.correct_answer: Các request đồng thời vẫn có thể chèn dữ liệu trùng lặp.
questions.explanation: Application checks alone are not enough under concurrency. Important uniqueness should usually be enforced in the database as well.
questions.code_snippet: ALTER TABLE users ADD CONSTRAINT users_email_key UNIQUE (email);
compare: App-only uniqueness check: race-prone.
Database constraint: stronger integrity protection.
questions.question_en: What is the trap in checking uniqueness only in application code before insert and not enforcing it in the database?
questions.question_vi: Trap in checking uniqueness only in application code before insert and not enforcing it in the database là gì?
questions.correct_answer_en: A. Concurrent requests can still insert duplicates.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Các request đồng thời vẫn có thể chèn dữ liệu trùng lặp.
questions.explanation_en: Application checks alone are not enough under concurrency. Important uniqueness should usually be enforced in the database as well.
questions.explanation_vi: Application checks alone are not enough under concurrency. Important uniqueness should usually be enforced in the database as well.
options_en: A. Concurrent requests can still insert duplicates.
B. Databases reject duplicates automatically without constraints.
C. Application checks are always stronger than constraints.
options_vi: Các request đồng thời vẫn có thể chèn dữ liệu trùng lặp.
B. Databases reject duplicates automatically without constraints.
C. Application checks are always stronger than constraints.
compare_en: App-only uniqueness check: race-prone.
Database constraint: stronger integrity protection.
compare_vi: App-only uniqueness check: race-prone.
Database constraint: stronger integrity protection.

questions.question: Trong trường hợp in request-scoped code, cách nào thường an toàn hơn: one long-lived session toàn cục hay a session được kiểm soát per unit of work?
options: Session được kiểm soát per đơn vị công việc is usually safer.
B. One session toàn cục is always best bởi vì it saves memory.
C. ORM sessions never hold state between operations.
questions.correct_answer: Session được kiểm soát per đơn vị công việc is usually safer.
questions.explanation: Global long-lived sessions can accumulate cũ state, transaction confusion, and cross-request leakage.
compare: Scoped session: clearer lifecycle and isolation.
Global session: convenient at first, fragile later.
questions.question_en: Which is usually safer in request-scoped code: one long-lived global session or a controlled session per unit of work?
questions.question_vi: Trong trường hợp in request-scoped code, cách nào thường an toàn hơn: one long-lived session toàn cục hay a session được kiểm soát per unit of work?
questions.correct_answer_en: A. A controlled session per unit of work is usually safer.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Session được kiểm soát per đơn vị công việc is usually safer.
questions.explanation_en: Global long-lived sessions can accumulate stale state, transaction confusion, and cross-request leakage.
questions.explanation_vi: Global long-lived sessions can accumulate cũ state, transaction confusion, and cross-request leakage.
options_en: A. A controlled session per unit of work is usually safer.
B. One global session is always best because it saves memory.
C. ORM sessions never hold state between operations.
options_vi: Session được kiểm soát per đơn vị công việc is usually safer.
B. One session toàn cục is always best bởi vì it saves memory.
C. ORM sessions never hold state between operations.
compare_en: Scoped session: clearer lifecycle and isolation.
Global session: convenient at first, fragile later.
compare_vi: Scoped session: clearer lifecycle and isolation.
Global session: convenient at first, fragile later.

questions.question: Vì sao cache invalidation hard in backend systems?
options: Bởi vì you must balance freshness, correctness, and efficiency.
B. Bởi vì caches delete themselves correctly by default.
C. Bởi vì invalidation only matters in frontend apps.
questions.correct_answer: Bởi vì you must balance freshness, correctness, and efficiency.
questions.explanation: Phần khó của caching không phải là lưu giá trị, mà là giữ cho chúng đủ chính xác so với hành vi thực tế của sản phẩm.
compare: No invalidation strategy: cũ data risk.
Deliberate invalidation: more complexity, more correctness.
questions.question_en: Why is cache invalidation hard in backend systems?
questions.question_vi: Vì sao cache invalidation hard in backend systems?
questions.correct_answer_en: A. Because you must balance freshness, correctness, and efficiency.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Bởi vì you must balance freshness, correctness, and efficiency.
questions.explanation_en: The hard part of caching is not storing values, but keeping them accurate enough for real product behavior.
questions.explanation_vi: Phần khó của caching không phải là lưu giá trị, mà là giữ cho chúng đủ chính xác so với hành vi thực tế của sản phẩm.
options_en: A. Because you must balance freshness, correctness, and efficiency.
B. Because caches delete themselves correctly by default.
C. Because invalidation only matters in frontend apps.
options_vi: Bởi vì you must balance freshness, correctness, and efficiency.
B. Bởi vì caches delete themselves correctly by default.
C. Bởi vì invalidation only matters in frontend apps.
compare_en: No invalidation strategy: stale data risk.
Deliberate invalidation: more complexity, more correctness.
compare_vi: No invalidation strategy: cũ data risk.
Deliberate invalidation: more complexity, more correctness.

questions.question: Which is usually a better optimization target first for a slow endpoint: tiny syntax tweaks or measuring query count and latency sources?
options: Measure query count and latency sources first.
B. Micro-optimize variable names and indentation first.
C. Add more comments before measuring anything.
questions.correct_answer: Measure query count and latency sources first.
questions.explanation: Performance work should start with evidence. Real bottlenecks are often I/O, query shape, or repeated remote calls, not tiny syntax choices.
compare: Evidence-driven tuning: better ROI.
Blind micro-optimization: low confidence and often wasted effort.
questions.question_en: Which is usually a better optimization target first for a slow endpoint: tiny syntax tweaks or measuring query count and latency sources?
questions.question_vi: Which is usually a better optimization target first for a slow endpoint: tiny syntax tweaks or measuring query count and latency sources?
questions.correct_answer_en: A. Measure query count and latency sources first.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Measure query count and latency sources first.
questions.explanation_en: Performance work should start with evidence. Real bottlenecks are often I/O, query shape, or repeated remote calls, not tiny syntax choices.
questions.explanation_vi: Performance work should start with evidence. Real bottlenecks are often I/O, query shape, or repeated remote calls, not tiny syntax choices.
options_en: A. Measure query count and latency sources first.
B. Micro-optimize variable names and indentation first.
C. Add more comments before measuring anything.
options_vi: Measure query count and latency sources first.
B. Micro-optimize variable names and indentation first.
C. Add more comments before measuring anything.
compare_en: Evidence-driven tuning: better ROI.
Blind micro-optimization: low confidence and often wasted effort.
compare_vi: Evidence-driven tuning: better ROI.
Blind micro-optimization: low confidence and often wasted effort.

questions.question: Vì sao coupling an HTTP request directly to a slow external provider hurt backend reliability có thể xảy ra?
options: Provider slowness can tie up request latency and resources end-to-end.
B. HTTP servers automatically turn slow providers into async queues.
C. Slow providers only affect frontend rendering.
questions.correct_answer: Provider slowness can tie up request latency and resources end-to-end.
questions.explanation: Sometimes the better design is a background job, timeout, fallback, cache, or asynchronous workflow rather than a synchronous dependency on the hot path.
compare: Synchronous dependency on hot path: simpler, but riskier.
Async or buffered design: more resilient under slow dependencies.
questions.question_en: Why can coupling an HTTP request directly to a slow external provider hurt backend reliability?
questions.question_vi: Vì sao coupling an HTTP request directly to a slow external provider hurt backend reliability có thể xảy ra?
questions.correct_answer_en: A. Provider slowness can tie up request latency and resources end-to-end.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Provider slowness can tie up request latency and resources end-to-end.
questions.explanation_en: Sometimes the better design is a background job, timeout, fallback, cache, or asynchronous workflow rather than a synchronous dependency on the hot path.
questions.explanation_vi: Sometimes the better design is a background job, timeout, fallback, cache, or asynchronous workflow rather than a synchronous dependency on the hot path.
options_en: A. Provider slowness can tie up request latency and resources end-to-end.
B. HTTP servers automatically turn slow providers into async queues.
C. Slow providers only affect frontend rendering.
options_vi: Provider slowness can tie up request latency and resources end-to-end.
B. HTTP servers automatically turn slow providers into async queues.
C. Slow providers only affect frontend rendering.
compare_en: Synchronous dependency on hot path: simpler, but riskier.
Async or buffered design: more resilient under slow dependencies.
compare_vi: Synchronous dependency on hot path: simpler, but riskier.
Async or buffered design: more resilient under slow dependencies.

title_en: Backend and Data Interview Question Bank
subtitle_en: Intermediate backend questions about APIs, persistence, validation, and reliability.
